phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: Slight edge at level 1: your root-drain sustain beat Anivia's wall-frost poke control on the margins. Take the short trades you start — just don't force a coin-flip all-in off a small lead.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: At level 2 the numbers lean your way — root-drain sustain edge out the wall-frost poke control when both sides play it straight. Press the small advantages: wave position, first poke, cooldown ticks.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: Slight edge at level 3: your root-drain sustain beat Anivia's wall-frost poke control on the margins. Take the short trades you start — just don't force a coin-flip all-in off a small lead.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Through levels 4-5 the numbers lean your way — root-drain sustain edge out the wall-frost poke control when both sides play it straight. Press the small advantages: wave position, first poke, cooldown ticks.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: Demonic Ascension online — you are now a drain-tank mage who WANTS the all-in everyone else fears. Invite the dive.

phases.label: First item
phases.why: On first item the numbers lean his way — the wall-frost poke control out-tick your root-drain sustain in even exchanges. Don't bleed for pride; take the trades his cooldowns hand you.

phases.label: 2+ items
phases.why: Slight deficit at two-plus items: Anivia's wall-frost poke control edge out your root-drain sustain on the margins. You can still trade — just let him start the exchanges and answer on your terms.

phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: At level 1 your root-drain sustain beat the stardust ramp control in a straight exchange. Play the front foot — make Aurelion Sol answer you, not the other way around.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: Your root-drain sustain out-muscle Aurelion Sol's stardust ramp control at level 2 — this window is yours, so force the trades and tax every step toward the wave.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: At level 3 your root-drain sustain beat the stardust ramp control in a straight exchange. Play the front foot — make Aurelion Sol answer you, not the other way around.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Slight edge through levels 4-5: your root-drain sustain beat Aurelion Sol's stardust ramp control on the margins. Take the short trades you start — just don't force a coin-flip all-in off a small lead.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: At the level-6 breakpoint the numbers lean your way — root-drain sustain edge out the stardust ramp control when both sides play it straight. Press the small advantages: wave position, first poke, cooldown ticks.

phases.label: First item
phases.why: Liandry's spike: the drain outpaces their damage in any extended fight. Force rivers, root the entry, ascend, profit.

phases.label: 2+ items
phases.why: Late Swain is the anti-team: flank-root their engage, ascend in their backline, and demonflare the clump that forms around you.

phases.label: Level 1

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: At level 2 the numbers lean your way — root-drain sustain edge out the soldier-poke shuffle control when both sides play it straight. Press the small advantages: wave position, first poke, cooldown ticks.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: W completes the setup: vision + slow from across the map. Root-into-W never misses, and the passive soul fragments heal you.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Lost Chapter timing — Q-spam stops costing the lane. Poke the wave contact and pull every rooted target with the passive.

phases.label: Level 6

phases.why: At two-plus items your root-drain sustain beat the point-click spear bullying in a straight exchange. Play the front foot — make Pantheon answer you, not the other way around.

phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: At level 1 the numbers lean your way — root-drain sustain edge out the windwall dash-weaving when both sides play it straight. Press the small advantages: wave position, first poke, cooldown ticks.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: E online: the root that defines the lane. Land it and the Q follow-up chunks; miss it and you farm for fourteen seconds.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: W completes the setup: vision + slow from across the map. Root-into-W never misses, and the passive soul fragments heal you.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Lost Chapter timing — Q-spam stops costing the lane. Poke the wave contact and pull every rooted target with the passive.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: Demonic Ascension online — you are now a drain-tank mage who WANTS the all-in everyone else fears. Invite the dive.

phases.label: First item
phases.why: Slight deficit on first item: Yasuo's windwall dash-weaving edge out your root-drain sustain on the margins. You can still trade — just let him start the exchanges and answer on your terms.

phases.label: 2+ items
phases.why: At two-plus items the numbers lean his way — the windwall dash-weaving out-tick your root-drain sustain in even exchanges. Don't bleed for pride; take the trades his cooldowns hand you.

phases.why: Late Swain is the anti-team: flank-root their engage, ascend in their backline, and demonflare the clump that forms around you.
